wikipedia.title: Captive portal
wikipedia.extract: A captive portal is a web page accessed with a web browser that is displayed to newly connected users of a Wi-Fi or wired network before they are granted broader access to network resources. Captive portals are commonly used to present a landing or log-in page which may require authentication, payment, acceptance of an end-user license agreement/acceptable use policy, or survey completion. Captive portals are used for a broad range of mobile and pedestrian broadband services – including cable and both commercially provided Wi-Fi and home hotspots. cves.description: GitLab before 5.4.2, Community Edition before 6.2.4, and Enterprise Edition before 6.2.1, when using a MySQL backend, allows remote attackers to impersonate arbitrary users and bypass authentication v
An authentication issue was discovered in GitLab that allowed a bypass of email verification. This was addressed in GitLab 12.1.2 and 12.0.4.

An issue was discovered in GitLab Community and Enterprise Edition 11.9 and later through 12.0.2. GitLab Snippets were vulnerable to an authorization issue that allowed unauthorized users to add comme

synthesis: GitLab's vulnerability history paints a concerning picture.  The existence of multiple CVEs, spanning authentication bypasses of varying severity, suggests a persistent security flaw in the platform.  The fact that these exploits can be used to bypass email verification, even with two-factor enabled, raises questions about the efficacy of the platform's security measures.  This raises a crucial question: are these vulnerabilities being exploited for malicious purposes, or is this a symptom of a broader security gap within GitLab's infrastructure?
